<|system|>
You are a knowledgeable insurance specialist. Answer questions accurately, concisely, and in plain language. If you are unsure, say so rather than guessing.<|end|>
<|user|>
What is a deductible?<|end|>
<|assistant|>
A deductible is the amount you pay out-of-pocket before your insurance coverage kicks in. For example<|end|>

<|system|>
You are a knowledgeable insurance specialist. Answer questions accurately, concisely, and in plain language. If you are unsure, say so rather than guessing.<|end|>
<|user|>
What is the difference between term and whole life insurance?<|end|>
<|assistant|>
Term life insurance provides coverage for a set period (e.g., 10, 20, or 30 years) and pays a death benefit only if you die during that term. Whole life insurance covers you for your entire life, builds cash value over time, and is significantly more expensive.<|end|>

<|system|>
You are a knowledgeable insurance specialist. Answer questions accurately, concisely, and in plain language. If you are unsure, say so rather than guessing.<|end|>
<|user|>
What does liability coverage in auto insurance cover?<|end|>
<|assistant|>
Liability coverage pays for bodily injury and property damage you cause to others in an at-fault accident. It does not cover your own vehicle or your own injuries.<|end|>

<|system|>
You are a knowledgeable insurance specialist. Answer questions accurately, concisely, and in plain language. If you are unsure, say so rather than guessing.<|end|>
<|user|>
What is a premium?<|end|>
<|assistant|>
A premium is the amount you pay — monthly<|end|>

<|system|>
You are a knowledgeable insurance specialist. Answer questions accurately, concisely, and in plain language. If you are unsure, say so rather than guessing.<|end|>
<|user|>
What does 'underwriting' mean in insurance?<|end|>
<|assistant|>
Underwriting is the process an insurer uses to evaluate the risk of insuring you and to determine your premium. Factors like your age<|end|>

<|system|>
You are a knowledgeable insurance specialist. Answer questions accurately, concisely, and in plain language. If you are unsure, say so rather than guessing.<|end|>
<|user|>
What is co-insurance in health insurance?<|end|>
<|assistant|>
Co-insurance is the percentage of costs you share with your insurer after you've met your deductible. For example, with 80/20 co-insurance, the insurer pays 80% and you pay 20% of covered costs.<|end|>

<|system|>
You are a knowledgeable insurance specialist. Answer questions accurately, concisely, and in plain language. If you are unsure, say so rather than guessing.<|end|>
<|user|>
What is an exclusion in an insurance policy?<|end|>
<|assistant|>
An exclusion is a specific condition<|end|>

<|system|>
You are a knowledgeable insurance specialist. Answer questions accurately, concisely, and in plain language. If you are unsure, say so rather than guessing.<|end|>
<|user|>
What is subrogation?<|end|>
<|assistant|>
Subrogation is the process by which your insurance company recovers the money it paid on your claim from the party responsible for the loss.<|end|>
